Title: Malaysia to send diplomatic note to Philippines over Sabah claim
Article:
REMBAU: Malaysia will send a diplomatic note to the Philippines to reject and protest any attempts to revive claims over Sabah, following remarks by Philippine Senator Robin Padilla.
Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said Wisma Putra would dispatch the note soon to reiterate Malaysia's firm stance.
"We will send a diplomatic note to state our protest and rejection of those statements. What they have stated is baseless because Sabah is an integral part of the Federation of Malaysia.
"The people of Sabah themselves, through the Cobbold Commission, chose to join the Federation of Malaysia, which was subsequently recognised by the United Nations.
"Therefore, the question of Sabah still being subject to their claim does not arise," he told reporters at his Aidilfitri open house in Kampung Tanjong Rantau today.
In a Facebook statement on Wednesday, Padilla reportedly said it was time for Manila to act firmly to "claim rights" over the territory, including sending official representatives to the area. He urged President Ferdinand Marcos Jr to revive the country's claim over the state.
Mohamad, who is also the Rembau member of parliament, said the issue was not new and suggested it was being recycled by certain politicians to divert attention from domestic problems.
"I believe the people of the Philippines themselves understand this. It is only their politicians who occasionally seek out issues to shift the public's focus away from actual problems.
"If they want to speak, let them. Some want to use this as a political campaign or to champion an issue to cover up their own weaknesses," he said.
Asked if the repeated rhetoric would strain ties between the two nations, Mohamad said bilateral relations remained intact as the remarks came from individual leaders rather than being the official stance of the Philippine government.
"I am confident that despite these claims, it has never soured the good relations between Malaysia and the Philippines, especially as the Philippines is the Asean chair this year," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Riding out negative impact of Mideast conflict
Article:
LETTERS: The escalating conflict involving Iran, the United States, and Israel is beginning to ripple across the global economy, with Malaysia not spared from its effects.
The impact is transmitted through higher oil prices, trade disruptions, financial market volatility, and rising living costs.
The effects are mixed. Certain sectors, particularly those linked to energy and commodities, may benefit in the short term. However, rising costs and uncertainty pose a more pressing challenge.
Higher oil prices are driving up transportation, production and import costs, particularly for food and industrial inputs.
These increases are gradually passed on to consumers, contributing to inflationary pressures.
While the oil and gas sector, along with commodities such as palm oil, may gain from elevated global prices, the broader domestic effect leans towards higher costs for households and businesses.
In financial markets, conditions have become more cautious. Heightened geopolitical tensions typically prompt global investors to adopt a risk-averse stance, leading to more volatile capital flows.
That said, Malaysia's position as a net energy exporter provides some support to the ringgit. The currency is expected to remain relatively stable, although with more pronounced fluctuations than usual.
Rising costs may influence business decisions, with some firms slowing hiring or holding back on wage increases. As a result, the key concern for households is not only employment, but also the erosion of real purchasing power.
In the property market, the effects are more indirect. Higher construction and financing costs may lead developers to delay new projects, while buyers become more cautious in an uncertain economic environment.
This could slow market activity, although it does not necessarily imply a sharp decline in property prices.
From an external perspective, Malaysia's current account could improve in the short term, supported by stronger export earnings from oil and other commodities. However, this advantage may be offset by higher import costs, particularly for food and industrial inputs.
However, the capital account is expected to become more volatile. In times of global uncertainty, short-term capital outflows may occur as investors reduce exposure to emerging markets.
Even so, Malaysia's relatively diversified economic structure helps cushion against more severe financial stress.
The country's diversified economic base provides a degree of resilience in absorbing external shocks compared to more narrowly structured economies.
In terms of the exchange rate, higher oil prices offer support, while cautious global sentiment exerts downward pressure. The result — the ringgit remains broadly stable but susceptible to short-term volatility.
The conflict does not pose an immediate crisis for Malaysia, but it does create a more challenging economic environment.
The key issue is no longer just growth, but how to balance emerging export opportunities with rising cost pressures on households.
Thus, targeted and data-driven policy measures, along with stronger support for vulnerable groups, will be essential to ensure that economic stability is maintained while safeguarding inclusiveness.
PROFESSOR DR ASAN ALI GOLAM HASSAN
Azman Hashim International Business School
Universiti Teknologi Malaysia
The views expressed in this article are the author's own and do not necessarily reflect those of the New Straits Times.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SWCorp Pahang issues 238 notices for littering, 19 face court action
Article:
TEMERLOH: SWCorp Pahang has issued 238 notices since the enforcement of the Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Act 2007 (Act 672) began on March 1.
SWCorp Pahang director Cairul Hisham Jalaluddin said 19 individuals have faced court action in Raub, Temerloh, and Kuantan.
Today, 12 offenders underwent community service orders for periods ranging from one to four hours, starting at 9am.
"In this instance, all offenders are aged 18 and above, with the oldest being 37," he said at the third series of the SWCorp Pahang PKM programme at Taman Tasik Bandar Temerloh today.
Cairul Hisham said SWCorp allows offenders to apply to serve their community service at locations near their homes to ease logistics and reduce travel costs.
"We do not want to burden them by forcing a return to the scene of the offence. They can apply to undergo community service where they reside," he said.
He noted that nearly 90 per cent of recorded cases involved discarding cigarette butts in public places.
"I urge smokers to find rubbish bins and dispose of them properly. Do not let a cigarette butt lead to a RM2,000 fine and up to 12 hours of community service," he said.
One offender, known only as Amir, 31, said he was caught while holidaying in Langkawi, Kedah, despite being from Kuantan.
"I threw a cigarette butt after failing to find a bin. I did not expect such enforcement, but I accept the legal consequences," said Amir, who served four hours of community service.
Another offender, Azizi Ramadan, 19, said he was cited after littering at the Temerloh Esplanade near Sungai Pahang on Jan 1.
"I have learned my lesson and will not repeat the mistake. I advise the public not to litter, including cigarette butts, as it dirties the area and invites legal action," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Twice in a month: Lawyer caught driving Ford Mustang without road tax
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 27-year-old lawyer was caught driving a Ford Mustang without road tax or insurance coverage for the second time in a month yesterday.
Kuala Lumpur Road Transport Department director Hamidi Adam said the man was stopped in the city centre while on his way home.
"Investigations also found that the man was driving with an expired licence."
"The car does not belong to him. The owner claimed he had just obtained a quotation to renew the road tax," Hamidi said in a statement, adding that the vehicle was seized.
Last year, Transport Minister Anthony Loke revealed that several
prominent figures
, including politicians, businessmen and even recipients of the "Tan Sri" title, were among vehicle owners who had failed to pay their road tax, with arrears amounting to RM35.7 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Road closure in Maran for Panguni Uthiram festival
Article:
KUANTAN: The main road in front of the Sri Maranthandavar Temple in Sungai Jerik, Maran, and its surrounding areas will be closed from March 30 to April 1 for the Panguni Uthiram festival.
The Maran district police, in a Facebook post, said the closure will take effect from 4pm on March 30 until 2pm on April 1.
Motorists are advised to plan their journeys and use alternative routes to avoid congestion.
Access to the main road remains available via Jalan Simpang Bangas, continuing to Jalan Utama and Bandar Jengka.
Police have urged the public to cooperate to ensure smooth traffic flow and safety throughout the annual celebration.
For enquiries, the public can contact the district police headquarters operations room at 09-4771222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heatwave: Ministry rolls out four key measures to protect rice supply
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Agriculture and Food Security Ministry has outlined four key initiatives to address the impact of the ongoing heatwave and supply uncertainties stemming from conflicts in the Middle East.
The measures include the implementation of the dry direct seeding method for padi, increasing the national rice buffer stock, conducting cloud seeding operations, and providing financial aid to affected farmers.
Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said that to mitigate the effects of the hot weather and boost dam levels, the ministry, through the Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada), will roll out the dry direct seeding technique.
"We are also working on cloud seeding operations in hopes of inducing rain over the dam catchment areas.
"Furthermore, affected farmers will be assessed for assistance under the Padi Crop Takaful Scheme," he said yesterday.
The dry direct seeding method involves tilling the soil while dry before sowing seeds on the field surface. Typically used during the first planting season, the technique significantly reduces water consumption.
Mada chairman Datuk Dr Ismail Salleh previously reported that the prolonged drought and extreme heat could cause padi yields to drop by up to 30 per cent.
Ismail noted that high temperatures interfere with critical processes in padi formation. Mada oversees nearly 83,000 hectares of padi fields in Kedah.
To ensure proactive future planning, Mohamad said the ministry's post-cabinet meeting last week approved the establishment of the National Padi and Rice Industry Consultative Committee (JKPIPB).
He described the committee as an official platform bringing together industry players, including farmer representatives, associations, and non-governmental organisations, to resolve industry issues collectively and transparently.
Meanwhile, to ensure the national rice supply remains stable amidst global uncertainty, Mohamad said the government will increase the national rice buffer stock by 100,000 metric tonnes through Padiberas Nasional Bhd.
This increase will bring the total national rice buffer stock to 300,000 metric tonnes, he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nga proposes Malaysia-Cambridge urban platform to drive SDG 2030 goals
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Housing and Local Government Ministry is moving to establish a Malaysia-Cambridge urban platform to accelerate the nation's Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) 2030 under the Madani economy framework.
Minister Nga Kor Ming said the proposed collaboration with the University of Cambridge aims to strengthen strategic partnerships with global institutions to drive sustainable urban development.
"Malaysia's strong economic growth and improving global competitiveness reflect our resilience as a formidable trading economy in Asia. It is timely for the country to step up efforts to establish strategic partnerships with the world's top institutions," he said in a statement today.
Nga earlier delivered a keynote address at the inaugural Forum on Housing and Urban Planning at Downing College, University of Cambridge, becoming the first minister from the Madani government to speak at the prestigious institution.
He outlined five key priorities under Malaysia's housing agenda, including strengthening the National Affordable Housing Policy by capping prices at RM300,000 to align with district median incomes.
Nga noted that Malaysia's home ownership rate has reached 76.5 per cent, surpassing developed nations such as Germany, the United Kingdom, and Australia.
Other priorities include advancing climate-resilient housing through transit-oriented development, enhancing planning via a national urban observatory, and reviving abandoned housing projects.
Nga revealed that nearly 1,350 abandoned projects have been revived since 2023, benefiting over 167,000 homebuyers and unlocking RM133.78 billion in value.
He added that the government is targeting "zero" sick and abandoned private housing projects by 2030 through five Madani housing reforms.
The minister also highlighted Malaysia's strong macroeconomic fundamentals under Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, citing a 6.3 per cent Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth in the final quarter of 2025 and record approved investments of RM426.7 billion.
The forum was jointly organised by the Rehda Institute, University of Cambridge, Anglia Ruskin University, and the Cambridge University Malaysian Students Association.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia enforces social media ban for under-16s
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia has officially banned social media use for children under the age of 16, marking the start of its landmark digital safety regulation, PP Tunas.
The Tata Kelola untuk Anak Aman dan Sehat Digital (Tunas) policy, which mandates the phased deactivation of underage accounts on high-risk platforms, makes Indonesia the first non-Western country to enforce such a nationwide restriction.
The country's Communications and Digital Affairs Minister Meutya Hafid said the move reflects Indonesia's commitment to digital sovereignty and child protection, following a one-year transition period since the regulation was signed by President Prabowo Subianto.
She noted that electronic system operators (PSEs) were given sufficient time to align with the rules, including submitting compliance commitments and action plans.
"Based on the compliance scale, we can convey that status is dynamic. This reflects the status as of tonight, and we are still awaiting developments until tomorrow," she said during a press conference last night.
As of 9.30pm Friday, Meutya confirmed that X and Bigo Live had demonstrated full compliance.
X has raised its minimum age requirement to 16 and updated its community guidelines, while Bigo Live increased its age classification to 18+, implementing layered moderation using artificial intelligence and human verification.
Two other platforms, Roblox and TikTok, were assessed as partially compliant and have been urged to complete their obligations promptly.
Roblox has proposed limiting users under 13 to offline use, while TikTok has committed to gradually deactivating accounts of users below 16 and will announce an operational roadmap for users aged 14 to 15.
Meutya stressed there would be no compromise on compliance, reiterating that all digital platforms operating in Indonesia must adhere to national laws.
"Every business entity operating in Indonesia must comply with the applicable regulations here," she emphasised.
Addressing comparisons with other jurisdictions like Australia, Meutya stated that child protection standards must be applied universally.
"Children in Indonesia are just as valuable as children anywhere else. There should be no situation where child protection rules are followed in some countries but not in others," she said.
On data protection, she said the regulation aims to safeguard children's personal data, which is currently widely exposed and exploited for monetisation.
The ministry said the rollout of PP Tunas will be conducted in stages, starting with major platforms, with enforcement to be expanded progressively.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested for selling fireworks at Terengganu Drawbridge with expired permit
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: A 36-year-old man was arrested for selling firecrackers and fireworks without a valid permit at Dataran Drawbridge early this morning.
The suspect was detained at 12.33am by police officers patrolling the popular tourist spot.
Kuala Terengganu police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the man was found openly selling the items to the public.
"Further inspection revealed the suspect did not possess a valid license. His permit to sell fireworks had expired on the sixth day of Hari Raya, yet he continued the activity illegally," he said when contacted today.
Police seized various types of fireworks and firecrackers valued at RM4,000.
"Initial investigations found that the suspect admitted to selling and possessing the fireworks without a license," Azli said, adding that the man tested negative for drugs and has no prior criminal record.
The case is being investigated under Section 8 of the Explosives Act 1957. The suspect was taken to the Kuala Terengganu magistrate's court this morning to be remanded.
Azli also issued a stern warning against individuals setting off fireworks in public areas, noting that such actions cause public anxiety and concern.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Melaka dam levels dip up to 30pct, but supply remains stable until April
Article:
ALOR GAJAH: Water levels at Melaka's three main dams have dropped by between 10 and 30 per cent due to the ongoing dry spell and lack of rainfall.
State Works, Infrastructure, Public Facilities and Transport Committee chairman Datuk Hameed Mytheen Kunju Basheer, however, assured that levels remain within the normal range, with supply expected to be sufficient until April.
"Based on current reports, the Durian Tunggal Dam is at approximately 70 per cent, Jus Dam at 60 per cent, and Asahan Dam at 90 per cent.
"The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has forecast high rainfall in the interior of the west coast, which covers the state's water catchment areas," he said at the Ayer Limau state constituency 'Aidilfitri Melaka Sayang Rakyat' event last night.
Hameed added that several contingency measures have been activated to safeguard the state's supply, including pumping water from Sungai Gerisik in Johor to the Merlimau water treatment plant.
Retention ponds across the state have also been put on standby to channel water to the dams if necessary.
MetMalaysia recently warned that the current hot weather is expected to persist until the start of the Southwest Monsoon in June, characterised by drier atmospheric conditions and reduced rainfall.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan rice yields to plunge 40pct due to prolonged drought
Article:
KOTA BARU: Rice yields in Kelantan are expected to drop by up to 40 per cent this season due to a prolonged drought.
Kelantan Farmers Association (Pesak) chairman Zuha Ismail said existing water supplies are insufficient to irrigate the affected padi fields.
He said the drought impacts 26,000 hectares of land worked by over 5,000 farmers.
The current crop is aged between 85 and 120 days, with some farmers already beginning the harvesting process.
"However, most of the crop will be harvested next month. Since the sowing process was already hit by water supply issues, we expect yields to decrease by 40 per cent.
"While there is water in the canals, the pumping capacity is insufficient to reach the fields, leaving some areas parched," he said when contacted.
Zuha added that following the sharp rise in diesel prices, some farmers are considering postponing the first planting season of the year, scheduled for June.
In light of rising costs, the association is urging the government to reconsider the current padi purchase price of RM1,500 per tonne.
"Many of us have decided to delay the new season because we are worried about the increasing operational costs following the diesel price hike. Farmers are waiting to see what approach the government takes, including a potential increase in the purchase price," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Silent killer: Miros warns against sleeping in cars with engine running
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sleeping in a car with the engine and air conditioning running, even for a short nap, can be fatal due to the silent threat of carbon monoxide (CO) poisoning.
Malaysian Institute of Road Safety Research (Miros) director-general Associate Professor Dr Siti Zaharah Ishak warned that CO gas can seep into the cabin through leaks in the engine bay or exhaust system.
The risk increases significantly in enclosed or poorly ventilated areas, such as multi-storey or underground car parks, where exhaust fumes accumulate and raise CO concentrations around the vehicle.
"The primary danger is that CO is odourless and colourless. It reduces the blood's ability to carry oxygen, leading to symptoms like headaches, dizziness, nausea, and unusual drowsiness, which are often mistaken for simple exhaustion," she told Berita Harian.
Siti Zaharah clarified that keeping windows closed does not guarantee a fully airtight cabin as CO can enter through gaps in door seals, the floorboard, the boot, or the air conditioning intake system.
"Modern vehicles are still at risk despite better sealing and airflow designs. Risks rise if components fail, unauthorised modifications are made, or maintenance is neglected," she said.
"The time it takes for an individual to lose consciousness depends on CO concentration, exposure duration, and the individual's health. In high-concentration scenarios, such as an enclosed space with an exhaust leak, the effects can manifest rapidly.
"If you experience early symptoms like dizziness, turn off the engine immediately, get out into an open area, and seek medical attention if symptoms persist," she advised.
She added that environmental factors like rain or being parked in a basement can cause exhaust gases to swirl back toward the vehicle's air intake.
While Miros does not yet have specific formal guidelines on sleeping in vehicles with the engine running, the public is strongly advised to avoid the practice.
"The main preventive step is to never sleep with the engine on. If you must rest, choose an open area with good ventilation, avoid enclosed parking, and do not leave the engine idling continuously," she said.
Siti Zaharah suggested that local authorities could play a role by ensuring functional ventilation in parking facilities and installing warning signs against prolonged idling.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KPDN steps up enforcement to curb smuggling, safeguard fuel supply
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) will continue to tighten enforcement to curb leakages and smuggling, while ensuring the smooth supply of controlled goods amid geopolitical uncertainties arising from the conflict in the Middle East.
KPDN Enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said the ministry's commitment is in line with the prime minister's call to ensure the sustainability of the nation's energy supply to meet the needs of the people.
As the ministry responsible for the supply chain of controlled goods, he said KPDN is formulating more aggressive strategies to address potential disruptions in the global fuel supply that could directly impact the economy.
"Several strategic measures will be implemented to ensure supplies remain accessible to the public.
"In line with energy sustainability initiatives, effective April 1, KPDN will fully enforce the ban on the sale and purchase of RON95 petrol by foreign-registered vehicles.
"This is to ensure fuel subsidies continue to benefit Malaysians. Any foreign vehicle owners or petrol station operators violating the regulation will face action under existing laws," he said in a statement today.
Azman said that from the same date, KPDN has also directed all petrol station licensees in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan to implement diesel sales controls for land transport vehicles based on categories, with purchase limits of 50 litres, 100 litres and 150 litres per transaction.
"This is crucial to ensure subsidised diesel, priced at RM2.15 per litre in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan, is not misappropriated by syndicates or ineligible industrial sectors.
"In terms of enforcement, some 2,400 KPDN enforcement officers nationwide are on alert to carry out inspections and monitoring," he said.
He added that under Ops Tiris 4.0, KPDN will enhance integrated strategic cooperation with the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency, police and other enforcement agencies to close gaps.
"Any attempt by individuals or syndicates to exploit the current global conflict will not be tolerated.
"Malaysians – consumers, traders, industry players and organisations – are urged to support the Kita Gempur (Eradicating Manipulation and Leakage) initiative," he said.
The public can channel complaints or information via WhatsApp at 019-848 8000, the KPDN e-Aduan portal, the call centre at 1-800-886-800, or the Ez ADU KPDN mobile application.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Traffic builds up on major highways as holidaymakers return
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic on several major highways nationwide began to build up as of 11am today as holidaymakers return from the Hari Raya Aidilfitri break.
A Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) spokesperson said congestion is currently concentrated along key stretches of the North-South Expressway (Plus) and within the Klang Valley, while traffic on the East Coast Expressway (LPT) remains manageable.
The spike in volume is also attributed to families returning ahead of the new school term, which begins this Monday.
"On the North-South Expressway (E1), northbound traffic is slow from Permatang Pauh to Sungai Dua, while southbound congestion is reported from Juru Auto City to the Juru Toll Plaza and from Changkat Jering to Kuala Kangsar.
"On the E2 stretch, northbound traffic is slow from Senai Utara to Kulai, while southbound lanes are congested from Putra Mahkota to Nilai Utara," he said when contacted.
Congestion was also reported at the Johor Causeway, with slow-moving traffic in both directions between the Woodlands Bridge midpoint and the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) in Johor Baru.
On the North-South Expressway Central Link (Elite), southbound traffic is crawling from Bandar Serenia to Nilai Utara.
Several major routes in the Klang Valley are also experiencing delays, including the Cheras-Kajang Expressway (GrandSaga) from Taman Suntex to Taman Connaught, and the Damansara-Puchong Expressway (LDP) from Puchong Utama to Puchong Intan.
Motorists are advised to plan their journeys, ensure vehicles are roadworthy, and maintain sufficient balances in their Touch 'n Go cards or e-wallets.
For the latest updates, the public can contact the LLM hotline at 1-800-88-7752, check the LLM Info Trafik WhatsApp, or use the Tuju and MyJalan applications.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested over cooking gas cylinder theft
Article:
MIRI: Police have arrested a man over the theft of cooking gas cylinders following a raid at Jalan Padang Kerbau yesterday evening.
Miri police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Farhan Lee said the case is being probed under Section 379 of the Penal Code for theft, an offence punishable by imprisonment for up to seven years, a fine, or both.
Police have also advised the public to remain vigilant and report any criminal activities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: The truth is out there: US vice-president 'obsessed' with UFOs
Article:
WASHINGTON: US Vice President JD Vance vowed Friday to plumb US government information about unidentified flying objects, adding that in his view, aliens are in fact demons.
"I have not been able to spend enough time on this to really understand it, but I am going to, trust me. I'm obsessed with this," a laughing Vance told the conservative podcaster Benny Johnson.
"I've still got three more years as vice president. I will get to the bottom of the UFO files," he added.
Johnson had asked about President Donald Trump's order to federal agencies in February to begin "identifying and releasing" government files related to UFOs and aliens.
Trump said he was taking the step "based on the tremendous interest shown," the same month that former president Barack Obama said publicly that aliens were "real," though he had not seen them.
Regarding those statements, Trump told reporters that Obama "gave classified information, he is not supposed to be doing that."
Vance for his part told Johnson on Friday, "I don't think they're aliens. I think they're demons," describing this as a Christian understanding of "celestial beings who fly around who do weird things to people."
No evidence has been produced of intelligent life beyond Earth.
Interest in UFOs and and related phenomena has been renewed in recent years as the US government investigated numerous reports of seemingly supernatural aircraft, amid worries that adversaries could be testing highly advanced technologies.
In March 2024, the Pentagon released a report saying it had no proof that unidentified aerial phenomena were alien technology, with many suspicious sightings turning out to be merely weather balloons, spy planes, satellites and other normal activity. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Call for special BNPL act to shield youths from spiralling debt
Article:
​​​​​KUALA LUMPUR: The enactment of a special act governing "Buy Now Pay Later" (BNPL) schemes is critical to protecting consumers, particularly the younger generation, from spiralling debt.
Global Law Thinker Society (GLTS) Malaysia chairman Muhammad Nazrin Faiz said the move is essential to balance consumer needs with the interests of traders.
"The law is still being adapted to BNPL because it has not yet been determined where the legal framework should be placed.
"There needs to be a clear platform so that consumers know their purchase limits. At the same time, sellers also know the costs or interest rates that should be charged," he said yesterday.
He said the enactment could also benefit both parties where traders can continue to generate profits, while consumers can spend without facing financial risks.
Muhammad Nazrin also suggested that stakeholders, including non-governmental organisations (NGOs), intensify financial literacy campaigns for the public.
"When we table the results of these programmes and move towards drafting the law, we are not just looking after traders but also protecting consumers.
"This will have a positive impact on the country's economy. Excessive debt will ultimately impact the nation," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No injuries in rooftop explosion, fire in downtown Toronto
Article:
TORONTO: A pair of explosions on the roof of a downtown Toronto building prompted an emergency response on Friday but no one was injured, officials said.
Emergency crews responded after two explosions were heard and a billowing plume of smoke could be seen rising from a six-story commercial building in a densely populated area close to the city's financial district.
Deepak Chagger, a spokesperson with Toronto Fire Services, said the fire broke out in an area of the roof where construction work was taking place. He said three propane cylinders had ruptured as a result of the fire.
Fire crews extinguished the fire and there were no injuries reported, Chagger said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: HR Ministry plans protection for over 400k Malaysians working in Singapore
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Resources Ministry will propose a Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) scheme to the cabinet to protect more than 400,000 Malaysians commuting to Singapore for work.
Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the "Skim Kembara" initiative is part of a broader effort to strengthen social protection, which includes a study in its final stages to extend coverage to women in confinement.
"This is our ministry's commitment to strengthening the country's workforce, in line with the Madani government's aspirations led by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim," he said in a statement.
Marking his 100th day in office, Ramanan said he is focused on ensuring policies improve lives through social protection, skills development, and worker welfare.
Key initiatives include the Skim Lindung 24/7 for Perkeso contributors, which is expected to benefit nearly 10 million formal workers nationwide by providing around-the-clock protection.
"In this short period, we have prioritised comprehensive and long-term initiatives rather than just temporary solutions," he said.
The ministry is also offering free occupational safety and health training to 10,000 industry workers in conjunction with Visit Malaysia 2026.
To bolster welfare for former security personnel, Perkeso has signed a memorandum of understanding with the Armed Forces Ex-Servicemen Affairs Corporation (Perhebat) and the Veterans Affairs Department.
Ramanan said the collaboration aims to secure job placements for 5,000 veterans through the MyFutureJobs Wira Career Protection Programme by 2030.
The ministry is also focusing on talent development through upskilling and reskilling under the Labour Department via the Centre for Advanced Technology, which is expected to benefit 90,000 people.
"These initiatives are vital to ensure the national workforce remains relevant. Our goal is not just to provide job opportunities, but to ensure our veterans earn a dignified income commensurate with their expertise and high discipline," he said.
During his first 100 days, Ramanan also launched the Vetri Madani 2026 Skills Programme, a high-impact initiative to empower the Indian community through technical, artificial intelligence, and engineering training alongside job placements.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: One parked car brings traffic to a halt as trailer gets stuck
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A single illegally parked car was all it took to bring an entire stretch of road to a standstill, sparking outrage online.
A viral video shared by the X account
Updateinfo
shows a long trailer transporting a massive excavator stuck at a tight bend, unable to move forward or reverse.
The obstruction was caused by a black car abandoned on the road shoulder, right at the curve, effectively choking the passage for the heavy vehicle.
The footage captures the logistical nightmare: the trailer, which required a wide turning radius due to its size and load, was trapped as the car narrowed the road to a single lane.
Within minutes, traffic began piling up in both directions, with frustrated motorists left stranded in a standstill jam.
Some drivers were seen stepping out of their vehicles, attempting to assess the situation, while others honked in vain, a chorus of irritation directed at a problem that could have easily been avoided.
What has particularly struck a nerve online is the blatant disregard shown by the owner of the black car.
Parking at a bend is not just inconsiderate, but it is also dangerous. In this case, it disrupted traffic flow entirely, affecting dozens, if not hundreds, of road users.
Netizens were quick to express their anger, with many pointing out that this was far from an isolated incident.
Irresponsible and haphazard parking, especially along narrow roads and critical roads like bends and junctions, is becoming increasingly common.
The incident highlights a persistent lack of civic responsibility among certain motorists who treat public roads as personal parking spaces.
Under existing regulations, vehicle owners who obstruct traffic can face stiff penalties, yet many believe
stricter enforcement
is needed to deter such repeat offences.
Until then, scenes like this may continue to play out, where one poorly parked car is all it takes to bring an entire road network to a standstill.
LINK:
https://x.com/update11111/status/2037606220065087843
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Social media age limit a 'digital seatbelt' for Malaysian youth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The implementation of a minimum age of 16 for social media use in the second half of this year has been hailed as a critical "digital safeguard" for the well-being of young people.
Universiti Utara Malaysia (UUM) School of Multimedia Technology and Communication dean Associate Professor Dr Mohd Khairie Ahmad said the initiative is a strategic step towards fostering holistic development while ensuring a safer online environment.
He noted that the measure complements the government's earlier efforts requiring social media platforms to obtain operating licenses starting January 2025, aimed at enhancing service providers' accountability for user safety.
"This move ensures that social media providers operate more responsibly, prioritising the safety and well-being of their users. Restricting those under 16 from registering or maintaining social media accounts is like a 'digital seatbelt,' offering an added layer of protection for young users," he said.
Dr Mohd Khairie, also a researcher at Universiti Utara Malaysia's (UUM) Advanced Communication Research Unit (ACRU), said the need for such controls has become increasingly urgent following several tragic incidents involving teenagers influenced by harmful content and unhealthy interactions on social media.
From a psychological perspective, he explained that the brains of children and early adolescents are still developing and therefore not yet fully equipped to manage impulses or accurately assess long-term risks.
"In other words, children and early teens are highly susceptible to environmental influences due to their heightened neuroplasticity. This makes them vulnerable to negative mental health impacts, cyber victimisation, involvement in online bullying, and potential issues with self-image and low self-esteem," he said.
To ensure the effectiveness of the policy, he suggested adopting user identity verification technologies (eKYC), similar to systems used in Australia and the European Union, which have proven effective in the banking and telecommunications sectors. He also recommended integrating Malaysia's MyDigital ID system, which offers strict control measures.
He explained that eKYC would verify users' identities through identity document scans, facial recognition, and biometric matching, while MyDigital ID could serve as an additional secure mechanism for verifying accounts on social media platforms, providing high levels of control and assurance.
"Social media platform providers must also take greater responsibility in enforcing this policy. Following Australia's example, platforms are required to prove that account holders meet the minimum age requirement.
"Failure to enforce age limits can result in heavy fines. In Australia, penalties can reach up to AUD50 million if platforms fail to take reasonable measures to prevent under-16 users from accessing their services," he said.
Khairie added that the government, social media providers and the telecommunications industry should strengthen digital literacy education for parents and guardians, including awareness campaigns and digital parenting programmes, to ensure the ecosystem fully supports the minimum age policy.
Parents need the knowledge and awareness to avoid unintentionally bypassing restrictions or creating fake accounts for their children, he noted.
Regarding Indonesia, which implemented social media restrictions for children under 16 starting today, he said the move is commendable and worth emulating, though Malaysia will adopt its own approach.
"Malaysia's age verification is expected to be stricter, initially using eKYC systems, compared with Indonesia's reliance on a combination of digital identity and AI-based age estimation," he said.
Indonesia is now the first Asian country to restrict access to high-risk digital platforms for children under 16.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US media: FBI director personal email hacked by Iran-linked group
Article:
WASHINGTON: An Iran-linked hacker group has accessed an email account of FBI Director Kash Patel and published personal photos and other material online, US media reported Friday.
CNN and other US media outlets, citing sources familiar with the breach, said the hack of Patel's email was claimed by the Handala Hack Team, a pro-Iranian hacktivist group.
According to CNN, the stolen emails include personal, business and travel correspondence and appear to date from 2011 to 2022, prior to Patel being named FBI director by President Donald Trump.
The FBI and Justice Department did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
The Justice Department warned following the Feb 28 launch of the US-Israeli war on Iran of potential cyberattacks by Iranian actors.
The FBI and Justice Department announced the seizure a week ago of four website domains that were allegedly used by hackers affiliated with Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security.
Among those seized were two domains allegedly used by Handala.
The Justice Department said the websites were used for "psychological operations targeting adversaries of the regime," posting stolen data and "calling for the killing of journalists, regime dissidents, and Israeli persons."
"Iran thought they could hide behind fake websites and keyboard threats to terrorize Americans and silence dissidents," Patel said in a March 19 statement announcing the seizure of the domains.
"This FBI will hunt down every actor behind these cowardly death threats and cyberattacks and will bring the full force of American law enforcement down on them," he added. — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Apple hires ex-Google executive to head AI marketing amid push to improve Siri
Article:
SAN FRANCISCO: Apple on Friday said it has hired Lilian Rincon, who previously spent nearly a decade at Google overseeing its shopping and assistant products, as the vice president of product marketing for artificial intelligence, reporting to its marketing chief Greg "Joz" Joswiak.
The hire comes as Apple is readying an improved version of Siri, its virtual assistant, for release this year, rebuilt with technology from Alphabet's Gemini AI model.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: More than 300 US troops injured since start of Iran war
Article:
WASHINGTON: More than 300 US troops have been wounded since the start of the Iran war on Feb 28, US Central Command said on Friday.
"Since the start of Operation Epic Fury, approximately 303 US service members have been wounded. The vast majority of these injuries have been minor, and 273 troops have returned to duty," US Navy Captain Tim Hawkins said.
A US official who asked not to be identified told AFP that 10 troops remain seriously wounded.
A further 13 troops have been killed in the war, according to the latest figures, with seven killed in the Gulf and six in Iraq.
In a separate development Friday, Iran's military said that hotels housing US soldiers in the region would be considered targets.
"When all the Americans (forces) go into a hotel, then from our perspective that hotel becomes American," armed forces spokesman Abolfazl Shekarchi told state television on Thursday.
Iran's government has not released an updated casualty toll, but a US-based activist group said on March 23 that some 1,167 Iranian troops had been killed and 658 troops' status is unknown. AFP is not able to independently verify tolls in Iran due to reporting restrictions.
The war began on February 28 when the United States and Israel launched strikes on Iran, killing its supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
Since then, the conflict has spread across the Middle East. Iran has fired drone and missiles at Gulf states home to American military bases and other interests.
US President Donald Trump insisted on Thursday that talks to end the conflict were "ongoing" and "going very well". - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Retired navy man nets RM6,000 monthly from backyard poultry farm
Article:
LUMUT: A retired Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) serviceman has transformed his backyard into a thriving micro-farm, proving that resourcefulness can break the cycle of poverty.
Hamdan Ahmad Sapli, 58, is earning up to RM6,000 a month by rearing laying hens and cultivating various crops.
He believes being part of the B40 group is not a permanent label.
"We must dare to start small at home. Even if space is limited, do not leave it idle. Take the first step, even if it is just five chickens. We must change our own circumstances," he said recently.
Hamdan began his journey in April 2022 during the Movement Control Order (MCO) after experiencing egg shortages.
Starting with just 30 hens purchased for RM7 each, he taught himself the basics of poultry farming through YouTube.
[[nid:1405123]]
By August 2022, his hens began producing eggs. Today, he manages more than 60 adult hens of various breeds including Australorp, White Leghorn, Coral, and Lohman Brown, producing about 30 eggs daily, and sold at 60 sen each.
Hamdan chose laying hens for their low maintenance and minimal space requirements.
"Each hen consumes about 110g to 120g of feed twice daily. I provide more feed in the afternoon as egg formation occurs at night," he said.
His farm features a unique integrated system where a chicken coop is built over a catfish pond. Droppings fall directly into the water to feed the fish, a method that also reduces odour and flies. The catfish are later used as a protein source for his poultry feed.
Beyond livestock, Hamdan cultivates kangkung, long beans, mustard greens, Brazilian spinach, pak choy, ginger, turmeric, lemongrass, and pandan. He also harvests honey from his stingless bee colonies every two months.
His initiative aligns with Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad's call for food security practices under the Perak Sejahtera 2030 Plan.
Hamdan's success has gone viral, attracting visitors from as far as the United States and Indonesia. Since 2023, he has shared his expertise with 6,000 participants through poultry and urban agriculture courses, with fees ranging from RM50 to RM100.
He also offers free educational visits for school students to raise awareness about food security. His efforts have garnered support and assistance from various government agencies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Colgate must face lawsuits over safety of mouth rinse for young children
Article:
WASHINGTON: A federal judge on Friday said Colgate-Palmolive must face two lawsuits claiming its packaging for mouth rinse misleads parents into believing children under 6 can use the products safely. The judge dismissed a similar lawsuit concerning Colgate toothpaste. Consumers in the proposed class actions said US health authorities warned that children under 6 should not use fluoride rinses, and recommended that children between 2 and 6 use only "pea-sized" amounts of fluoride toothpaste.
They said Colgate's packaging, featuring bright colors and flavors such as Bubble Fruit and Silly Strawberry, made it seem safe for young children to use rinses, and to use as much toothpaste as older children and adults. Fluoride can be harmful if swallowed.
US District Judge Andrea Wood in Chicago said reasonable consumers might not know where to draw the line for rinses, given how most Colgate labels prominently featured the words "kids" or "children's."
She said she was unpersuaded by Colgate's insistence that consumers would know rinses are over-the-counter drugs and check the back labels, which contain required US Food and Drug Administration warnings for young children.
Toothpaste was different, the judge said, because the labels expressly instructed that children between 2 and 6 use pea-sized amounts.
"Viewed in context, the toothbrush with a full strip of toothpaste is there only to represent the act of toothbrushing," Wood wrote.
Colgate, based in New York, and its lawyers did not immediately respond to requests for comment.
Michael Connett, a lawyer for the plaintiffs, said courts have been receptive to deceptive labeling claims. "These rulings will hopefully send a wake-up call to manufacturers to stop promoting unsafe use of fluoride products," he said.
Procter & Gamble, which makes Crest, as well as Perrigo and Sanofi have also been sued over their packaging of fluoride products for children. Colgate agreed last September to introduce new packaging for its Colgate, Tom's of Maine and hello brands of toothpaste to resolve an investigation by Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton. Procter & Gamble reached a similar resolution in January. - Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perak Mufti Department: 'Tidur Lambak' event may breach Islamic law
Article:
IPOH: The Perak Mufti Department has expressed concern over the "Tidur Lambak" event, citing risks of "ikhtilat" or the mingling of unrelated men and women, which could contravene Islamic law.
The two-day event, organised by Tapir Studio, is described as an immersive experience reimagining the communal sleeping arrangements typically found during "balik kampung" trips.
In a statement, the department said while it did not reject the event entirely, the programme must be reviewed to ensure compliance with Islamic principles and Malaysian cultural values.
It said such scrutiny was vital to prevent slander against Islam and to preserve the morality of the Malay community.
"Among the risks that may arise include the difficulty of guarding one's gaze, the accidental exposure of aurat, and physical contact between those who are not mahram, which must be avoided," the statement read.
The department advised the organisers to refine the programme's implementation and consult religious authorities before proceeding.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Night Zoo Johor draws record 7,330 visitors on opening night
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Night Zoo Johor saw a massive turnout on its opening night yesterday, with 7,330 visitors experiencing the state's newest attraction.
State Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor said the figures surpassed initial expectations of 5,000 visitors.
The night zoo features 35 additional species comprising 100 animals.
"In the first three hours alone, we recorded over 3,000 visitors. We are maintaining the most affordable ticket prices in Malaysia. Currently, we have 93 species and a total of 507 animals at Zoo Johor," he said when met at the launch here last night.
Night Zoo Johor operates from Friday to Sunday, 8pm to 11pm, with the last entry at 10pm.
Tickets are priced at RM10 for adults and RM5 for children over five and senior citizens aged 60 and above. For foreign tourists, the price is RM30. Entry is free for children under five and persons with disabilities.
Commenting on the facility's development, Mohd Jafni said the state government spent RM9 million on the first phase of upgrades and RM7 million on the second phase, bolstered by a RM1.4 million federal contribution for a new reptile house.
"For the second phase, we also upgraded walkways to be more friendly for those with disabilities.
"We will proceed with the third phase, which focuses on constructing an animal clinic, additional quarantine areas, and upgrading offices and staff quarters. An allocation of RM2 million has been approved for this phase," he said.
Visitor Muhammad Shah Fikri Sukarto, 24, a lorry driver from Kota Tinggi, said he travelled with his family to be among the first to experience the attraction.
"I came specifically for the night zoo, and the vibe is unique. My family no longer needs to travel to Melaka or other states to experience a zoo at night," he said.
Another visitor, Sylvia Thian Many Sia, 48, from Taman Desa Jaya, praised the zoo's cleanliness and aesthetics following its 2024 reopening.
"It has been a long time since I visited Zoo Johor. Seeing the animals at night is exciting because I realised many are actually more active then. I will definitely come back," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heatwave sparks surge in air conditioner sales as consumers seek relief
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The extreme heatwave currently sweeping the country has triggered a surge in air conditioner sales, with some units reportedly sold out as consumers scramble for relief.
A check by Harian Metro found that some desperate homeowners are purchasing up to five units at a time to cool their residences.
Pensioner Othman Majid, 70, described the current weather as unusual and urgent, prompting him to use his pension savings to install an additional unit in his home.
Othman said the decision was made after his eight-month-old grandson had to be taken back to his parents' house because he could not withstand the heat in the living room.
"The children are used to the cold. When they come to my house, it is so hot they have to take off their clothes. They cry non-stop because of the heat. As a result, I had to buy an air conditioner," he said.
Concerned over the cost of living and rising electricity bills, Othman opted for a unit with inverter technology to ensure energy savings.
The scorching weather has also proven to be a boon for electrical retailers.
Sales supervisor Zairol Hafizi Zalni, 34, said demand at his shop has spiked significantly from a daily average of just six or seven units.
"The highest sales we have ever recorded were only six to seven units a day. In fact, recently, a customer came and bought five units at once to be installed in all the rooms at his house.
"The majority of customers are looking at the price of cheap air conditioners, but we will advise them to take the inverter model. Although it is a little expensive, if installed for more than four hours, it saves consumers money in terms of the electricity bills," he said.
He also confirmed that the supply of air conditioners and prices remained stable without any sudden increase.
Another salesperson, Chin Siew Chin, 50, said that one-horsepower (1HP) air conditioners, along with other equipment such as fans and water filters, are the main targets of the middle class who are desperate to find a quick solution.
An air conditioning technician, known as Shamilnur, said his daily schedule has now started early, with the target of installing and repairing the units at five to eight houses a day.
"Now, many people are asking for urgent service because the machines might need some repairs.
"Some customers complain that they have set the temperature to the lowest, but the room is still not cold because of the extreme temperature outside that causes condensation, so I advise people to maintain their units regularly to make them last longer," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Merbok murder victims laid to rest
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: The couple who were found murdered and dismembered in their home in Kampung Belau, Merbok, were laid to rest last night.
The remains of Zikri Abdul Rahman, 65, and Suraya Man, 53, were buried at the Masjid Al Atiq Islamic Cemetery in Merbok at 9pm. The funeral was attended by about 300 people.
Earlier, the bodies were released from the Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital (HSB) Forensic Department in Alor Setar at 5.25pm following post-mortem examinations, before being taken to the mosque for funeral prayers.
It was previously reported that three family members were found dead in the house two days ago. Police and villagers, alerted by a
foul stench
, broke down the door at 12.30am.
The couple's bodies were discovered with their heads severed and limbs dismembered, placed on a carpet and covered with cloth in the kitchen.
Suraya's 29-year-old son was found dead in an upstairs bedroom of the double-storey house with slash wounds to his neck.
Police recovered several sharp weapons on the upper floor near the son's body.
All three bodies were sent to HSB for post-mortems, including deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) testing and fingerprinting for the couple.
The son's remains were claimed by the family yesterday and buried on the same day.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Housing Ministry eyes University of Cambridge tie-up on sustainable urban planning
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) is seeking to establish a Malaysia-Cambridge urban platform to pursue Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) 2030 under the Madani economy framework.
Its Minister, Nga Kor Ming, said the proposed collaboration with the University of Cambridge would strengthen strategic partnerships with leading global institutions in pursuit of sustainable urban development.
“Malaysia’s strong economic growth and improving global competitiveness reflect the nation’s resilience as a formidable trading economy in Asia despite ongoing geopolitical tensions, and it is timely for the country to step up efforts to establish strategic partnerships with the world’s top institutions,” he said in a statement today.
Nga earlier delivered a keynote address at the inaugural Forum on Housing and Urban Planning at Downing College, University of Cambridge, making him the first minister from the Madani government to be invited to speak at the prestigious institution.
He said Malaysia’s engagement with Cambridge reflects the government’s commitment to international collaboration, positioning the country as a proactive partner in developing affordable, resilient and inclusive cities.
The forum, jointly organised by the Real Estate and Housing Developers’ Association (Rehda) Institute, the University of Cambridge, Anglia Ruskin University and the Cambridge University Malaysian Students Association, also aims to encourage Malaysian students abroad to return and contribute to nation-building.
In his address, Nga outlined five key priorities under Malaysia’s housing and urban agenda aligned with the SDGs, including strengthening the National Affordable Housing Policy by capping prices at RM300,000 and aligning them with district median income.
He said Malaysia’s home ownership rate has reached 76.5 per cent, surpassing countries such as Germany, the United Kingdom and Australia.
Other priorities include advancing climate-resilient housing through transit-oriented development and nature-based solutions, enhancing land and planning coordination via a national urban observatory, and reviving abandoned housing projects.
Nga said nearly 1,350 abandoned projects have been revived since 2023, benefiting over 167,000 homebuyers and unlocking RM133.78 billion in value, while stressing that strong local authorities are key to building sustainable cities and enhancing institutional capacity through digitalisation, training and improved governance.
He added that through five Madani housing reforms, the government is targeting “zero” sick and abandoned private housing projects by 2030, while also strengthening the governance of high-density and strata living through reforms to the Strata Management Act.
Under the leadership of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Nga said Malaysia’s macroeconomic fundamentals have reached a turning point, with a 6.3 per cent Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth in the final quarter of 2025 and record approved investments of RM426.7 billion. — Bernama

Title: Traffic builds on major highways as Hari Raya travellers head home
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Traffic flow on several major highways nationwide has begun to build up and slow as of 11am today, as travellers make their way back after the Hari Raya Aidilfitri holiday.
A spokesperson for the Malaysian Highway Authority, when contacted by Bernama, said congestion is currently concentrated along key stretches of the North-South Expressway and within the Klang Valley, while traffic on the East Coast Expressway remains manageable.
The increase in volume is also attributed to families returning ahead of the new school session, which begins this Monday.
“On the North-South Expressway (E1), northbound traffic is slow from Permatang Pauh to Sungai Dua, while southbound congestion is reported from Juru Auto City to the Juru Toll Plaza and from Changkat Jering to Kuala Kangsar.
“Meanwhile, on the North-South Expressway (E2), traffic heading north is slow from Senai Utara to Kulai, while southbound lanes are congested from Putra Mahkota to Nilai Utara,” he said.
Congestion has also been reported at the Johor Causeway, with slow-moving traffic in both directions from the midpoint of the Woodlands Bridge to the Bangunan Sultan Iskandar in Johor Bahru, and vice versa.
On the North-South Expressway Central Link (ELITE), southbound traffic is moving slowly from Bandar Serenia to Nilai Utara.
Several major routes in the Klang Valley are also experiencing delays, including the Cheras-Kajang Expressway (GrandSaga), where northbound traffic is slow from Taman Suntex to Taman Connaught, as well as the Damansara-Puchong Expressway (LDP), with congestion from Puchong Utama to Puchong Intan.
Motorists are advised to plan their journeys carefully, ensure their vehicles are in good condition, and maintain sufficient balance in their Touch ‘n Go cards or e-wallets before travelling.
For the latest traffic updates, the public can contact the Malaysian Highway Authority hotline at 1-800-88-7752, check WhatsApp LLM Info Trafik, or use the TUJU and MYJalan applications, as well as LLM’s official social media channels on TikTok (@llmtrafik) and Facebook. — Bernama

Title: Concessionaire clarifies that fatal rider accident occurred on NPE, not LDP
Article:
PETALING JAYA, March 28 — Amanat Lebuhraya Rakyat Berhad (ALR), the concessionaire of the Damansara-Puchong Expresswa (LDP), has issued a clarification regarding a fatal accident involving a food delivery rider on Thursday, saying this did not occur on its expressway as previously reported.
In a statement, ALR confirmed that the accident took place at KM 3.0 of the New Pantai Expressway (NPE), on the stretch heading from Bandar Sunway towards Bangsar.
The clarification addresses
earlier reports
that cited Petaling Jaya deputy police chief Supt M. Hussin Sollehuddin Zolkifly as saying the LDP was the location of the incident.
In the accident, a 38-year-old food delivery rider was killed after his motorcycle crashed into a stalled car at approximately 1.40pm.

Title: Misusing subsidies a betrayal of the country, says KPDN as enforcement tightens
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, March 28 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) has issued a stern warning that any attempt by individuals or syndicates to take advantage of the current global conflict will not be tolerated.
Its Enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said misappropriating national subsidies is a betrayal of the people and the country.
“The strictest enforcement action will be taken with no compromise,” he said in a statement today.
He also called on all Malaysians, including consumers, traders, industry players and organisations, to support the KITA GEMPUR (Movement to Combat Manipulation and Leakages) initiative for the common good.
At the same time, 2,400 KPDN enforcement officers nationwide are on high alert to carry out inspections and monitoring activities on the ground.
“Through OPS TIRIS 4.0, KPDN will strengthen integrated strategic cooperation with the Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS), the police (PDRM) and other enforcement agencies to close any gaps that allow leakages and abuse,” he added.
In line with efforts to ensure energy sustainability, KPDN will fully enforce the ban on the sale and purchase of RON95 petrol for foreign-registered vehicles.
It will also implement controls on diesel sales for land transport vehicles based on category in Sabah, Sarawak and the Federal Territory of Labuan starting April 1.
The ministry will continue to tighten enforcement to curb leakages and smuggling, while ensuring stable supply of controlled goods despite geopolitical uncertainties arising from the escalating conflict in West Asia.
“This ongoing effort is in line with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s call to ensure the country’s energy supply remains secure and sufficient to meet public needs,” he said.
As the ministry responsible for the supply chain of controlled goods, KPDN has also outlined more aggressive strategies to address potential disruptions in global fuel supply that could affect the national economy.
Several strategic measures will be implemented to ensure supplies remain easily accessible to the public.
Members of the public can channel complaints or information through official platforms, including WhatsApp 019-848 8000, the KPDN e-Aduan portal (https://eaduan.kpdn.gov.my), the call centre at 1-800-886-800 and the Ez ADU KPDN application. — Bernama

Title: Three Melaka dams see levels drop due to heat, state assures supply until April
Article:
ALOR GAJAH, March 28 — Water levels at three dams in Melaka have dropped between 10 and 30 per cent due to hot weather and lack of rain recently.
State Works, Infrastructure, Public Facilities and Transport Committee chairman Datuk Hameed Mytheen Kunju Basheer, however, said that the water level remains at a normal reading and, so far, the water supply is expected to be sufficient for the state until April.
“Based on the report received, the Durian Tunggal Dam is at about 70 per cent level, the Jus Dam (about 60 per cent) and the Asahan Dam (about 90 per cent).
“Based on the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) report, high rainfall is expected in the interior of the west coast, covering water catchment areas in this state,” he told reporters after the Ayer Limau state assembly ‘Aidilfitri Melaka Sayang Rakyat’ event here last night.
He added that, so far, various initiatives had been implemented to ensure the state's water supply is not affected if the hot weather continues and there is no rainfall in the near future, including pumping water from Sungai Gerisik, Johor, to be channelled to the Merlimau water plant.
In addition, several retention ponds in the state have also been activated to channel water to the dam areas, if necessary.
Three days ago, Bernama reported that MetMalaysia said that the hot weather phenomenon currently affecting the country is expected to continue until the beginning of the Southwest Monsoon (MBD) in June, following drier atmospheric conditions and lower rainfall.
Earlier, Hameed said over 10,000 people attended the Aidilfitri event, which ran from 5pm until midnight.
Hameed, who is the Ayer Limau state assemblyman, said visitors to the open house were treated to over 30 types of food, including satay, chicken rendang, tomato rice, ayam golek and prawn rendang.
Also present were the Melaka Yang Dipertua Negeri, Tun Dr Mohd Ali Mohd Rustam, his wife Toh Puan Asmah Abd Rahman, Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh and his wife Datin Seri Zuriyah Ab Aziz. — Bernama

Title: Socso plan to cover cross-border workers commuting to Singapore in final stage, says HR minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — The Ministry of Human Resources is in the final stages of submitting a proposal to the Cabinet to introduce a Traveller Scheme under the Social Security Organisation (Socso), aimed at protecting more than 400,000 Malaysians who commute daily to work in Singapore.
Minister R. Ramanan said the initiative is part of broader efforts to strengthen the country’s social safety net.
“In addition, we are also finalising a study to extend social protection to mothers during childbirth,” he said in a statement yesterday.
Reflecting on his first 100 days in office, Ramanan said Kesuma has prioritised policies that deliver direct and long-term benefits to the people, particularly in social protection, skills development and worker welfare.
“In this short period, our focus has been on comprehensive and sustainable initiatives, not just temporary measures,” he said, adding that the efforts align with the Madani government’s vision led by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Among key milestones is the tabling of the 24/7 Protection Scheme under Socso in Parliament, which is expected to benefit nearly 10 million formal workers nationwide.
To support the tourism sector, Kesuma has also rolled out free Occupational Safety and Health (SITI) training for 10,000 industry workers in conjunction with Visit Malaysia Year 2026.
In addition, SOCSO has signed a memorandum of understanding with PERHEBAT and the Department of Veterans Affairs to create an integrated ecosystem for Malaysian Armed Forces veterans, targeting 5,000 job placements through the MYFutureJobs Wira Career Protection Programme by 2030, he said.
Ramanan said talent development remains a key priority, with upskilling and reskilling initiatives under the Department of Manpower (JTM) through the Advanced Technology Training Centres (Adtec) expected to benefit 90,000 individuals.
He also welcomed the RM100 million allocation announced by the Prime Minister to support gig workers’ skills training through the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPK), describing it as recognition of the growing gig economy.
Meanwhile, the VETRI Madani 2026 Skills Programme, valued at RM10 million and supported by HRD Corp and TalentCorp, has been launched to provide training in technical fields, artificial intelligence and engineering, along with job placement opportunities for the Indian B40 community. — Bernama

Title: Level 2 heatwave hits Kedah, Perlis and Hulu Perak, says MetMalaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Five districts in Kedah, Hulu Perak in Perak and the whole of Perlis recorded a Level 2 heatwave, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia).
Based on the updated records on the official website of MetMalaysia as of 6am today, the affected districts in Kedah are Baling, Sik, Pendang, Pokok Sena and Kota Setar.
Meanwhile, several other areas in Kedah — Bandar Baharu, Kulim, Kuala Muda, Padang Terap, Kubang Pasu and Langkawi — are under Level 1 (alert) status.
Also under Level 1 are Perak Tengah, Kinta, Kuala Kangsar and Selama in Perak; the northeast and Northern District in Penang; Jerantut in Pahang; and Lubok Antu in Sarawak.
A Level 2 heatwave is declared when the daily maximum temperatures range from 37 to 40 degrees Celsius for at least three consecutive days, while Level 1 refers to daily temperatures between 35 and 37 degrees Celsius over the same period. — Bernama

Title: #BellaSyedYes: Syed Saddiq and Bella Astillah confirm engagement, after proposal on Mount Kinabalu (VIDEO)
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — After months of playful denials, coy social media posts, and a trail of clues that kept fans on the edge of their seats, it appears that Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman and actress-singer Bella Astillah have finally taken the next big step in their relationship.
Syed Saddiq posted a video on his social media showing him at what seems to be Mount Kinabalu alongside Bella, both dressed in purple, with the caption: “I asked, she said yes,” with the hashtag #BellaSyedYes, a playful pun on their combined names.
He also confirmed on social media that an official engagement reception would be held today.
The speculation had reached its peak on March 27 when Syed Saddiq's brother, influencer Space Dollah (Syed Abdullah Syed Abdul Rahman), seemingly confirmed the engagement during a live-streaming session.
In a moment of excitement, Space Dollah was caught on video saying to Bella: “Saya hargai awak. Saya sayang awak. Selamat datang ke…ke. Baru nak cakap selamat datang ke keluarga, eh.”* (I appreciate you. I love you. Welcome to… to. I was about to say welcome to the family) .
When Bella sent a gift during the live stream, an overwhelmed Space Dollah added that he had been keeping her secret, telling viewers that he had to leave it to the couple to announce their own news . The video quickly went viral, with fans celebrating what they viewed as the long-awaited confirmation that their beloved "BASS" (Bella Astillah Syed Saddiq) were officially tying the knot.
The road to this moment has been anything but quiet. Rumors of a romance first began swirling in 2024 when the pair were announced as brand ambassadors for a clothing line. What started as a professional collaboration soon blossomed into a close friendship that captured the public's imagination .
Even Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim was overheard joking with Bella about whether he would receive a wedding invitation. Bella's response? "Insya-Allah"
For now, Syed Saddiq and Bella Astillah remain characteristically tight-lipped. But as the Muar MP himself said earlier this year: “Insya-Allah, this year will be a very meaningful and special year.”
It seems that promise is about to be fulfilled.
https://www.facebook.com/reel/1261766789394837/

Title: Police rescue 14, arrest seven in Melaka human trafficking raids
Article:
MELAKA, March 28 — Police detained seven individuals for alleged involvement in human trafficking and rescued 14 others believed to be victims of forced labour here today.
Melaka Police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said two raids were carried out by a team from the D3 Anti-Trafficking in Persons and Anti-Smuggling of Migrants (ATIPSOM) Division, Criminal Investigation Department (CID) from Bukit Aman, at Taman Klebang Besar and Jalan Tengkera here at about 1am.
“In the operation, seven suspects aged between 19 and 38 were detained, comprising a man from China, a Taiwanese woman, a Myanmar man, and four women from Myanmar, the Philippines and Indonesia.
“All suspects were arrested under Section 12 of the ATIPSOM Act 2007 for allegedly being involved in forced labour exploitation activities, and are also being investigated under Section 6(1)© of the Immigration Act 1959/63,” he said in a statement today.
Dzulkhairi said that in the same operation, police also rescued 14 victims aged between 25 and 36, comprising seven men and six women from the Philippines, as well as one woman from Myanmar.
He said initial investigations found that all the victims were believed to have been trafficked for forced labour exploitation in online scam activities.
“The victims were initially promised jobs as customer service personnel, but upon arriving in the country, they were forced to be involved in the scam activities.
“They were also believed to have been subjected to debt bondage, with their passports confiscated by the management, placed under strict control and not allowed to leave, and were even forced to work overtime against their will,” he said.
Dzulkhairi added that all those arrested, as well as the victims, had been handed over to the investigating officer at the Melaka Tengah district police headquarters for further action.
“An application for an Interim Protection Order (IPO) for the 14 victims has been made for 21 days starting today until April 16, 2026,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Body of teenage boy found after suspected crocodile attack
Article:
KUCHING, March 28 — The body of a teenage boy who was suspected to have been a victim of a crocodile attack while bathing with his family along Sungai Sarawak in Kampung Segubang, Batu Kawa, was found this afternoon.
The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Operations Centre said that the body of the boy, 15, was found by family members during a search and rescue operation at about 5.30 pm not far from where he went missing.
“Before he went missing, the victim was with his siblings bathing in the river while his mother was washing clothes.
“The victim complained of pain from his leg before he was believed to have been dragged away by a crocodile. During the incident, the boy’s grandfather was at the location and chased after the boy, who was being dragged by the crocodile, with a boat,” the centre said in a statement yesterday, adding that the victim disappeared from sight about 200 metres from where he was attacked.
“The body of the victim was found without his left and right arms,” the centre said, adding that the body has been handed over to the police for further action. — Bernama

Title: Health Ministry aims to ease HKL MRI congestion with increased outsourcing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has reintroduced the Hospital Services Outsourcing Programme (HSOP) at Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL) to ease congestion and reduce waiting times for patients requiring magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) services.
In a statement yesterday, HKL said the HSOP reduced patient waiting times by 33 per cent in 2025, from 24 weeks to 16 weeks.
“In 2025, HKL optimised 96.8 per cent of its allocated slots, using 365 out of 377.
“For this year, the hospital has applied for 640 slots, a 70 per cent increase, to shorten patient waiting times even more,” read the statement.
The programme was officially relaunched on Wednesday (March 25) after the approval of new funding on March 19.
HKL said its Radiology Department operates two MRI machines in the Main Block, with capacity for 24 to 28 cases a day, depending on case complexity and patient condition.
“However, daily scans have sometimes reached 36 to prioritise emergency and critical cases that need urgent attention.
“HKL and the MOH remain committed to providing efficient healthcare services that meet the highest standards of care,” the statement said.
Previously, a portal reported that HKL’s limited MRI services had forced the hospital to refer patients to private facilities for scans. — Bernama

Title: Speeding trailer in Port Klang triggers viral chaos as container falls, creating traffic snarl
Article:
SHAH ALAM, March 28 — Traffic was congested along Jalan Kem in Port Klang, near here, yesterday after a container fell off a trailer believed to have been speeding.
South Klang district police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said the incident, which occurred at about noon, did not result in any casualties or damage to other vehicles.
“Initial investigations found that the trailer was believed to be speeding when passing through the location, causing the container to become detached from the trailer before falling onto the road.
“Other vehicles using the route managed to stop in time, avoiding a collision. Checks on the 30-year-old male driver found that he tested negative for alcohol and drugs,” he said in a statement today.
In the same statement, Lim warned drivers of commercial vehicles not to drive recklessly or they will face stern action, as such behaviour could endanger other road users.
The case is being investigated under Section 43(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
Earlier, a video of the incident went viral on social media. — Bernama

Title: Merbok murder victim couple laid to rest
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI, March 28 — The remains of a married couple believed murdered in Kampung Belau, Merbok, were laid to rest at 9pm last night at the Masjid Al-Atiq Muslim cemetery here.
Two Malaysian Armed Forces (ATM) vehicles carrying the remains of Zikri Abdul Rahman, 65, and his wife Suraya Man, 53, arrived at the mosque at 7 pm, after being claimed by their family from Hospital Sultanah Bahiyah in Alor Setar.
